name: cable one arm decline chest fly
instructions: Attach a D-handle to a low pulley cable machine and set the bench to a decline angle.
Lie down on the bench with your head towards the machine and grab the handle with your right hand.
Extend your arm straight up above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
With a controlled motion, lower your arm out to the side until your hand is in line with your shoulder.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the motion and bring your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your left arm and repeat the exercise.

Attach a D-handle to a low pulley cable machine and set the bench to a decline angle. Lie down on the bench with your head towards the machine and grab the handle with your right hand. Extend your arm straight up above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbow. With a controlled motion, lower your arm out to the side until your hand is in line with your shoulder. Pause for a moment, then reverse the motion and bring your arm back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your left arm and repeat the exercise.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
